actionUnits.condition: Ignition via neighbor influence. Target has BoxCollider (IsTrigger=0) and no Rigidbody. Attached MonoBehaviour (script guid=1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d; component fileID≈2592690533154406537 from prefab) is Fire. Serialized settings in scene match the Fire script: isOn=0, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, onNeighborsThreshold=2, maxNeighborDistance=4, downMultiplier=2, minNeighborDownDistance=3, maxHitInterval=2, totalHitTime=5, mainEffectGo=fileID 1849464941111748095. There are no UnityEvents (no m_Calls). To cover ignition code paths, ensure at least two neighbor Fire instances are ON and within 4 units; if one is 3+ units lower on Y, ignition countdown speeds up by downMultiplier. Also ensure neighbors list is populated (Fire.FindNeighbors called by controller) so onNeighbors counts and NotifyNeighborState flows. Expected behavior: shouldTurnOn becomes true, timeToOnDelta accumulates until >= timeToOn (random 40-60), isOn flips to true, UpdateFireState(true) plays particle system and notifies neighbors and FireController.Instance.FireChanged(+1).

Extinguishing via water hits. This object implements IWaterInteractable.WaterHit(Vector3) (called externally on water collision). Precondition: the fire must be ON (either from the previous neighbor-ignition step or by an external system calling SetIsOn(true)). During this trigger window, repeatedly hit the collider with the water stream so that each hit occurs within maxHitInterval (2s) to keep isTurningOff true; when cumulative currentHitTime reaches totalHitTime (5s), Fire turns off and UpdateFireState(true) stops the particle system and notifies neighbors and FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1). Note: There are no no-arg public methods to call directly; interaction must be via physics/water system invoking WaterHit.
